data.title: Nvidia Q4: Why Even A Record 'Beat' Could Sink The Stock
data.description:
Introduction

As we are headed into the last and most important earnings of the season, coming on February 25th, the market gets excited and worried at the same time. I can relate to the feeling, as I can say with confidence that Nvidia Corporation (NVDA) has started this AI boom, and it has the power to stop it if the numbers are disastrous. At the same time, I recognize that even if that happens and Nvidia disappoints the market, most likely it won’t be this quarter, and not even in 2026. Wall Street agrees with me, and you can see that by 36 upward EPS revisions, with only 1 negative prediction.

I am an Nvidia shareholder myself, and I seriously considered trimming my position ahead of the results, and of course, I decided to turn my decision-making process into this article. I will explain what the main metrics I am considering when taking a look at Nvidia are, what worries me, and what I am entirely confident about.

Ahead Of Earnings Sentiment Is The King

Nvidia is expected to deliver $65.6 billion in revenue, which is an increase of more than 60% from the $39 billion it made a year ago and also a significant increase from $57 billion quarter over quarter. While the numbers are incredibly high, and the growth rate is significant, I believe the market will react extremely negatively if the company really delivers those $65 billion. The reason here is the enormous Capex spending we saw throughout 2026.

As you can see on the graph from Goldman Sachs Research, the Capex spending until November 2025 has reached almost $400 billion, and Nvidia, with its products and services, eats up the majority of that Capex. For this reason, in reality, the market would like to see a beat of at least $2-$3 billion. Anything less will result in the stock decline.

And, funny enough, over the last year, on earnings or right after, this company has seen significant declines in the stock price, even though Nvidia consistently beats expectations by a wide margin. This is one of the reasons I don’t really like how Nvidia communicates with the shareholders.

The management leaves out the room to beat the expectations constantly. This stock has seen explosive growth a few years back for this reason as well. Of course, the innovation, the growth, and the margins were the main reasons, but the ability to deliver a “beat” every quarter added fuel to the rally. But on the flip side of this coin is the reaction of the market to the earnings from Nvidia nowadays. Analysts and investors are used to pricing in much more than the company guides for, and because of this, the earnings often get messy.

People do not expect better-than-expected results due to the increased revenues or some other objective metrics, but rather because “the company always beats,” “the Capex was so huge,” or “no way they are going to miss.” I realize that the quality of the analysis of Nvidia as a business up until this point in the article is poor, but the sentiment is one of the main factors I consider when I decide on whether I want to buy, hold, or sell, and I will return to the discussion of the sentiment in the conclusion. For now, let’s discuss the business and the market.

Nvidia What?

I hope that by this point, everyone knows that Nvidia stock is expensive for a very good reason. If we compare it to all of the Mag 7, Nvidia wins against those companies on every important metric. Revenue and Net Income growth leave most of the competitors far behind. Net Income, by the way, grows faster than the revenue, which is a great sign and points to the fact that the demand is organic, or at least it was in the last year. The company does not lower its margins to stay relevant, and it does not spend a fortune on marketing. It, by the way, could be partially attributed to the circular financing that worries all of the Nvidia shareholders, myself included, but we will come back to this in the Red Flags segment.

But if you want to compare it in any other relevant way, you would see that Nvidia is not even in the same category as the rest of the Mag 7 (I do not include Tesla (TSLA), just because it is far behind the rest of the companies, and the graph doesn’t look as good when I add it. You can read my latest Tesla piece here. Looking at the graph below, you would see that on Gross Profit Margin, Nvidia is almost the best one, but this is out of this world because Nvidia is effectively a hardware company. This is an unheard-of level, and this level would be solid for a software leader. Return on Invested Capital usually should be at least a few percentage points above the WACC (weighted average cost of capital). If that happens, we say that the company is allocating the capital successfully, which usually points to a prosperous business model, favorable market conditions, and correct decision-making from the management. Nvidia’s WACC is usually somewhere between 10-15%. This means that the company would look really good and solid if its ROI were at ~20%. Its ROI today is at 99%.

I could continue this list, but I explained all of this in detail in my previous article about Nvidia. There, I break down why this company and its results are not an AI bubble.

Remember, just in the last paragraph, I called Nvidia a hardware company? I have to take it back, because even though it is true, the main moat of Nvidia in AI is not even the GPUs. It is the whole ecosystem Nvidia has built around it.

They indeed produce the world’s most powerful chips, but so does almost every company in Mag 7 by now. Their own custom silicone might not be as strong, but it does the work perfectly and covers the needs of those enterprises. Focusing solely on the silicon when looking at Nvidia entirely misses the actual moat that makes Nvidia untouchable in the AI race. By wedding their hardware to the CUDA software platform, they’ve ensured that every major AI framework and developer is essentially working on Nvidia's coding language. For a competitor to win, they don’t just need a faster chip. They need to convince an entire generation of developers to move to a different way of working, and that is a much harder sell than just selling hardware. Think for a second about the whole large ecosystem that has to be transferred. This is really expensive, and in my opinion, senseless. That’s why companies, hyperscalers, can be powering themselves with their own production, but they won’t be able to entirely substitute Nvidia in their operations.

So, the next time you hear someone say Nvidia is losing relevance, ask that person to explain who exactly will be paying an insane amount of money to rebuild the entire infrastructure in the company, fire all of the developers, and hire the new ones who work with a different ecosystem. Then, ask them why someone would do that. Mag 7 companies build their chips to diversify, and to eliminate the risk of the sole supplier, but it doesn’t mean they want to cut Nvidia out. It would simply make no sense, as Nvidia has been working on this technology for over two decades, and no other company on the market can compete with that. Especially not Alphabet (GOOG) or Amazon (AMZN), which started making that silicone a few years ago.

Red Flags

I might look like I am overly positive regarding Nvidia, but I am really not. This company just has incredible results, and as of today, February 18th, 2026, it truly looks unbeatable and unstoppable. At the same time, there are a couple of red flags I never ignore, and those are the reason I considered trimming my position ahead of earnings.

The first red flag and the first major concern of the market is circular financing. We all know that Nvidia is a part of the scheme when it invests in different companies, and then, those companies become clients of Nvidia, basically buying their chips and adopting Nvidia's ecosystem. Now, this creates a fair question of whether those companies would at all work with Nvidia and have the money to work with Nvidia if Nvidia were not funding those companies. It really looks like Nvidia buys clients for itself, and this is definitely not a great position to be in. As a shareholder, I would definitely prefer Nvidia to grow organically, not pay for marketing more and more every quarter, not lower the prices, and definitely not pay the customers to buy its own products.

At the same time, I see why this might not be such a bad idea from Nvidia’s standpoint. If you take a look at Nvidia's business, it is incredibly strong; it’s resilient. The return on invested capital is really high. If the business works great, what is the best you can do if you have available cash (and Nvidia has a substantial cash position; it has nothing to do with that)? In this case, it sounds like a good decision to invest in companies that have nice prospects while “creating” customers for itself. I have no idea whether it will prove to be genius or disastrous as time passes, but for now, I am following this and paying attention.

Another red flag I noticed a couple of quarters ago is the inventory, which has been growing rapidly for the past few quarters, even though the new chip is not out yet, and the fastest part of the Blackwell cycle should be behind us. I did extensive research to explain this buildup, but there are so many different theories that it’s difficult to understand what the truth is and where the guesses of people start.

In my opinion, the main red flag is that management hasn't addressed this issue. They haven't clarified whether this is work-in-progress, finished products they can’t move, or perhaps H20 originally intended for China that are now sitting due to tariffs and waiting to be sold. We have no way of knowing for sure, and that lack of transparency makes me cautious.

For a hardware company, building up inventory isn't automatically a sign to run. Usually, it just means they’re bracing for a massive wave of orders. We saw exactly this with Nvidia between January and July 2024. Inventory went up from $5.2 billion to $9.2 billion, but then it went back down to $6.6 billion as those products were sold to customers. This proves that back then, the demand was real, and the company indeed was preparing for that demand beforehand.

But since then, the inventory of Nvidia has been growing at a pace that we have not seen before, even when the demand was actually enormous. Because my research hasn't yielded a definitive answer, I can't say for certain if this is a positive or negative sign. What I can say for sure is that management must address this on the next earnings call, and it is one of the main things I’ll be looking out for.

Valuation

Nvidia is pretty expensive at the moment, especially if you want it to be priced like other Mag7 stocks. It is trading at about 44x trailing 12-month earnings. Its 12-month average is usually closer to 47, which means it is currently trading below its normal valuation. At the same time, objectively, if it does much better than other players, it must be more expensive, right?

The fact that it is slightly cheaper than the valuation suggests investors are being cautious ahead of earnings, despite the high expectations and the wave of positive revisions from the market. Even though the valuation is slightly lower than usual, I suspect it could drop even further before the report or right after.

Given these incredibly high expectations, I’m not sure the market truly realizes what Nvidia has to do to deliver a real "beat." This is where the sentiment becomes a challenge. Nvidia has built a reputation for always over-delivering, and now the market expects a massive surprise. The problem is that no one knows exactly how large that beat needs to be to satisfy investors. "Better than expected" has become a moving target that stretches indefinitely, and there’s a risk that whatever Nvidia shows won't be enough. Because of this, I wouldn't call the stock cheap heading into earnings.

Of course, if we look at the PEG ratio and price in the growth expected for 2026, the picture changes. We can be fairly confident in that growth given the massive CapEx allocations from hyperscalers, with Nvidia as the primary benefactor. In that context, the stock looks objectively cheap relative to its performance. However, market fear is very significant. I’d argue that sentiment is currently the main negative catalyst. While a lower valuation might make people more willing to buy, Nvidia is performing so well that it’s hard to see the valuation dropping much further than this.

Conclusion

To conclude, is Nvidia a great company? Yes. Will the hyperscalers be able to substitute it? No. Can it withstand the competition? Of course. Nvidia is number one, and the market is large enough to support multiple players anyway. It doesn’t have to be the only one; there is a place for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD).

Are there red flags? Yes. I’ll be keeping a close eye on the stories surrounding circular refinancing and growing inventory, as these could point to issues further down the line. However, I don’t see those factors significantly impacting the company throughout 2026. My main concern this year is sentiment. I don’t think investors are ready to fuel another massive rally unless the stock drops to a level where people feel truly confident buying in again. Right now, the primary headwind is simply the market’s own fear. Whether those fears materialize in 2027 or beyond remains to be seen, but for now, Nvidia looks solid.

If I weren't already a shareholder, I wouldn't be buying ahead of earnings. The stock often dips after the report, so I’d either wait or split my entry between "before" and "after" just to be safe. Ultimately, Nvidia is the type of solid company you can dollar-cost average into over the long term. Even if the AI boom slows down, we will eventually see new hardware update cycles in three, five, or seven years. Nvidia will simply become a cyclical leader, dipping when the cycle cools, and surging whenever the companies start upgrading.

data.title: White Gold Focuses On Gold Production In Dawson City, Yukon
data.description:
Upward Momentum In White Gold: More Than Justified

Shares of White Gold Corp. (WHGOF), listed on the US OTCQX, have risen nearly 640% in the past twelve months and nearly 33% year-to-date, underscoring the high market confidence in this stock.

We believe this confidence is more than justified. This article explores the underlying facts that support our alignment with the market participants’ assessment and the shared vision of a buy signal.

About White Gold Corp. Part I: Maiden Gold Project In The Yukon, In Northwestern Canada

In one of its recent press releases regarding its participation in the Virtual Investor Conference for Precious Metals & Critical Minerals on February 12, 2026 (PMCM Virtual Investor Conference, February 12, 2026), White Gold Corp. describes itself as follows:

The Company owns a portfolio of 15,362 quartz claims across 21 properties covering 305,102 hectares (3,051 km 2) representing approximately 40% of the Yukon’s emerging White Gold District.

The Company’s flagship White Gold project hosts four near-surface gold deposits which collectively contain

resource estimate of 1,732,300 ounces of gold in indicated resources (35.2 million tonnes grading 1.53 grams per tonne gold) and 1,265,900 ounces of gold in inferred resources (32.2 million tonnes grading 1.22 g/t Au).

(see the Company's news release dated October 6, 2025)(3)(4).

About White Gold Part II: Enormous Exploration Potential, Known Regional Presence Nearby

In the press release for PMCM's virtual investor conference of February 12, 2026, White Gold states:

Regional exploration work has also produced several other new discoveries and prospective targets on the Company’s claim packages which border sizable gold discoveries including

the Coffee project owned by Fuerte Metals with Measured and Indicated Resources of 80.0.2 Mt grading 1.15 g/t Au for 2.96 million ounces of gold, and Inferred Resources of 21.2 Mt grading 1.17 g/t Au for 0.80 million ounces gold(8)(2)(4), and Western Copper and Gold Corporation’s Casino project which has Measured and Indicated Resources of 2,490.7 Mt grading 0.18 g/t Au, 0.14% Cu for 14.8 million ounces of gold and 7.6 billion pounds of copper, and Inferred Resources of 1,412.5 Mt grading 0.14 g/t Au, 0.10% Cu for 6.3 million ounces of gold and 3.1 billion pounds of copper(1)(2)(4).

For more information visit www.whitegoldcorp.ca.

Existing Deposits Near Golden Saddle, Arc, Ryan's Surprise And VG

Two important trends: The larger Ryan's Trend and Chris Creek Trend host numerous target areas and existing deposits near Golden Saddle, Arc, Ryan's Surprise and VG.

Expandable Resources Upon Successful Exploration

The project comprises four zones: Golden Saddle, Arc, Ryan's Surprise, and VG. There is potential for expansion along strike and at depth. The Golden Saddle zone's resources now exceed those of the other zones, with 1.1 million ounces (indicated) and 93,000 ounces (inferred). (See White Gold Corp.'s press release from October 6, 2025).

Resources Are Increasing

The significant increase in indicated (44%) and inferred (13.4%) resources for the flagship White Gold project in the Canadian Yukon, announced on October 6, 2025, suggests a positive impact of such announcements on the share price. These announcements demonstrate that the gold deposits are not merely empty promises in corporate presentations, and the share price reflects the strengthened investor confidence in the long-term prospects of White Gold Corp.

On October 6, 2025, Dylan Langille, Vice President of Exploration at White Gold Corp., commented on the resource achievement as follows:

The updated MRE on our flagship White Gold Project represents another key development for the continued growth and advancement of our resources, which now ranks as one of the highest-grade undeveloped open pittable gold deposits in Canada. Significant opportunities exist to continue to expand the resource size through further drilling on the resource itself, nearby targets, continued optimization of the resource block model and the Target for Further Exploration area which has seen limited exploration to date. We look forward to continuing to demonstrate the gold and critical mineral endowment of our prospective district scale land package in a top jurisdiction which has such a rich history and geological prospectivity.

Resources Are Becoming Known As High-Grade Discoveries

The discovery of a high-grade gold deposit at Golden Saddle is another success in exploration efforts. The positive share price performance following the announcement on November 4, 2025, confirms this. Such discoveries effectively contribute to long-term confidence in White Gold stock, especially as they are reinforced by the company's communications, reasonably introducing White Gold as a key player in the resource sector of the future. One example of this is the “ Inside the Boardroom” event hosted by CEO.CA on November 13, 2025.

“Inside the Boardroom,” Newsfile Corp. press release dated November 13, 2025, reported by Seeking Alpha, describes CEO.CA as:

[…] the leading investor social network in junior resource and venture stocks, shares exclusive updates with CEOs of junior mining explorers.

Investor Confidence Is Rising

This is signaled by White Gold Corp. recently closing a $23 million private placement, a fairly noteworthy capital raise compared to the historical issuance of common stock from White Gold Corp.

The capital raise in the private placement includes participation from global, major gold producer Agnico Eagle Mines Limited (AEM:CA) as well as the insiders of David D'Onofrio, CEO, and Dylan Langille, VP Exploration, and the company PowerOne Capital Corp., which acquired shares “ for investment purposes.”

More On High-Grade Gold Deposit At Golden Saddle

David D’Onofrio, Chief Executive Officer, commented on the November 4, 2025, PR about the high-grade gold discovery at Golden Saddle:

These high-grade results exceeded our expectations and further demonstrate the exceptional potential of our Flagship Project which already ranks as one of the highest-grade undeveloped open pittable gold deposits in Canada,” […] “Intersecting one of the best intervals ever reported from the property, while confirming additional mineralized zones in the hanging wall and footwall, underscores the robust nature of this deposit and is just one example of the significant opportunity to further expand and enhance our resource base.

And Dylan Langille, Vice President, Exploration at White Gold added:

[…] We are eagerly anticipating the results from the additional drilling and other exploration performed this year.

The core Golden Saddle zone contains 695,000 ounces of indicated and 35,000 ounces of inferred resources. Exploration is currently underway in a target area with an estimated 10–12 million tonnes of gold at grades of 1–2 g/t (corresponding to approximately 321,500 to 771,600 troy ounces). The resource size can be expanded by optimizing the deposit models. As most resources are located near the surface, they are ideally suited for open-pit mining.

Exploration Open Up New Avenues For Portfolio Diversification

Approximately 40 kilometers southeast of the White Gold project, in its 100%-owned Pedlar property, a significant copper multi-element porphyry deposit known as The Bridget Target is emerging, about 55 km southeast of Dawson City.

White Gold Corp.'s 2025 geophysical surveys at the Bridget target area provided strong evidence of porphyry mineralization. Geochemical findings reinforce the company's intention to advance its expansion goals through exploration. This promising mineralogical picture is due to the location of the Bridget target area within the Minto-Carmacks Copper Belt, near Western Copper and Gold's Casino deposit. This basin, with its gold and copper occurrences, is described in more detail in the “About White Gold Part II” section of this article. Also, the Pedlar project area, where the Bridget target area is located, is traversed by significant fault lines that host several mineral occurrences, including Western Copper and Gold's Casino deposit and Fuerte Metals' Coffee project. Information on the resources of the latter project can also be found in the “About White Gold Part II” section of this article.

David D’Onofrio, Chief Executive Officer of White Gold, commented on White Gold Corp.'s 2025 geophysical surveys, which are bringing new momentum to the porphyry target areas in the Bridget target area, as follows:

With global demand for critical minerals accelerating and Canada placing historic emphasis and support on development of domestic copper and critical minerals, the results at Bridget come at a pivotal time. Projects with scale potential in safe, mining-friendly jurisdictions are increasingly sought after, and Bridget is shaping up to be exactly that type of opportunity. The multiple targets we’ve outlined highlight the strength of our portfolio and our ability to advance a meaningful discovery in a region poised to play a major role in the future of Yukon and North American critical mineral supply chain. We look forward to advancing our critical mineral opportunities along with our flagship Gold Resource which represents one of highest grade open pittable deposits in Canada and continuing to make new gold & critical mineral discoveries on our district scale land package.

The Bridget Project: A Wild Card For Diversification To Electro-Copper - A Promising Future

The Bridget project seems like an absolutely logical and timely move, as it enables a connection between the need for profit and shareholder value creation and the growth opportunities that will arise from the project pipeline in a rapidly growing global demand for copper. This growth is driven by AI and defense applications, as well as the adoption of electrification, according to forecasts from consultancy firm S&P Global, reported by Seeking Alpha, also including that the consumption will increase from 28 million to 42 million units by 2040.

The supply is not expected to be sufficient to keep up with the demand. Due to these supply constraints, copper has been classified as a critical commodity in the United States, according to S&P Global, reported by Seeking Alpha, leading to upward pressure on copper futures. As a future copper producer, the prospect of a supply shortfall driving copper prices up is positive for the company's future earnings. We likely won't have to wait until after initial production to see the positive impact of electro-copper on market sentiment for the shares, as progress on the Bridget and Pedlar projects will already be priced in as the project pipeline makes headways. Market participants are internalizing the story of white gold and the associated copper boom, helpful for the shareholders reaping the benefits, probably also indirectly if, as we will show, this segment is spun out to an independent company in which White Gold still retains a stake.

The Strategic Moves Towards The Gold Production Goal

These are White Gold's strategic steps to achieve its gold production target.

Management And Strategic Partners' Board Strengthen

White Gold Corp. supports White Gold's growth in the Yukon with the appointment of Donovan Pollitt, a seasoned mining manager with more than 20 years of experience, as a strategic advisor.

Mr. Pollitt possesses unique expertise in mining permits, gold mine operations, and capital raising and is supporting White Gold in the next steps to advance its gold and key mineral projects. These consist of the release of further results from the 2025 drilling programs, the continuation of technical studies for a preliminary economic assessment for the company's flagship White Gold Project in the first half of 2026, and the completion of its largest exploration program to date for 2026.

The slide shows the board before the appointment of Mr. Pollitt.

Strategic Spin-Out Of Copper And Critical Metals

The spin-out of SpinCo, a new Yukon-focused exploration company for critical minerals, is a major step. White Gold is transferring copper, molybdenum, tungsten, and other critical mineral deposits to SpinCo.

In our view, this is an extremely smart move, as it allows White Gold shareholders to fully concentrate on gold. This strategy will enable White Gold to accelerate its development and capitalize on the rapidly rising gold price. At the same time, SpinCo, which is seeking a listing on the TSX Venture Exchange, will expand its market presence in areas that were previously part of White Gold Corp.'s original portfolio. These areas, which White Gold could not have fully showcased while focusing on gold, now stand to benefit from dedicated attention as a separate company. This move enhances the chances of securing financing for key copper-gold porphyry deposits such as Bridget, Isaac, and Mascot in the Dawson Range. Without this spin-out, it would be very unfortunate if the financing for these important mining projects were to fail or be delayed. This would mean missing out on the opportunity to drill at key locations such as Bridget, an area White Gold has identified as a promising exploration target.

Spin-Out: Extra Advantages For Shareholders

Under the “White Gold” name, these properties—which hold considerable potential for critical minerals such as copper, molybdenum, tungsten, and silver—would be overshadowed by the focus on gold mining. Unless they are transferred to a company specializing in their separate development, in this case SpinCo, the risk of being overlooked in the growing market for critical minerals is mitigated. While White Gold's gold holdings will continue to be closely monitored due to the precious metal's ongoing upward trend, shareholders now have the additional opportunity to invest in SpinCo through the Canadian market. We expect that SpinCo will eventually rename itself to better reflect the crucial importance of copper and other metals, thus optimally positioning itself for strong government support and the increasing demand for strategic minerals.

The Ownership Structure Can Reveal A Lot

The ownership structure appears to be as follows: Over 40% of the shares are held by institutional investors, White Gold Corp. insiders, high-net-worth individuals, and private equity and venture capital firms focused on growth in stocks. They may also retain a considerable stake in the remaining 57%, which is held in the “Public and Other” segment.

This is the structure where influential investors have an evident equity presence in White Gold through their holdings—ranging from privately held to publicly traded companies. That said, it seems unlikely that White Gold’s strategy, including the spin-out of its copper business and its critical metals division, has been misconstrued.

Look at the ownership structure, as from there the trend seems a well-delineated one, and it would be very unfortunate if participants in U.S. OTC trading of White Gold shares were not keeping up with it, in our view.

WHGOF White Gold Corp. On The US OTCQX

Shares of White Gold Corp., which trade on the US OTC market under the symbol “WHGOF,” were quoted at $1.18 per share, representing a market capitalization of $259.84 million.

Technical Analysis

The share price has fluctuated between $0.14 and $1.46 per share over the past 52 weeks. The average of this range is $0.80 per share. Currently, White Gold Corp.'s stock is trading 47.5% above this 52-week average. The 20-day moving average is above the 50-day moving average, which in turn is above the 100-day moving average, and this is above the 200-day moving average. These lines are logically arranged on the White Gold Corp. stock chart in the US OTC market. This suggests that the market and investors expect more and more of upward trend:

A) In the short term, until the overbought area is reached, the current RSI value of 56.03 offers plenty of room before a pause occurs or a short-term pullback to reposition in anticipation of the next upward trend.

B) Market participants also expect an upward trend in the medium and long term. This is a normal development that aligns with the company-level activity at White Gold Corp.

Considerations On Valuation

Since White Gold Corp.'s shares are traded over-the-counter in the US, the company does not produce gold and therefore generates no revenue, income, or cash flow by not selling it. Consequently, these balance sheet items are absent, precluding traditional valuation based on key figures and indices compared to other gold producers.

But White Gold Corp. has offered $23 million worth of common stock at a price of $0.85 per share in an expanded private placement. This price is below the current market price. This price of $0.85 should not be interpreted as an indicator of the company's intrinsic value if that's what you're thinking. The enhanced perception of gold as a safe haven during times of geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty has driven the share price up from $0.85 to $1.18. Industry analysts are optimistic about the future performance of the gold price. For example, analysts at ANZ Bank last week estimated that the Gold Spot Price (XAUUSD:CUR), currently at $4,885.27 per ounce, could reach $5,800 per ounce by the second quarter of 2026. Deutsche Bank also forecasts a price of $6,000 per ounce for 2026. White Gold continues to advance its exploration and resource development efforts. With experienced advisors like Donovan Pollitt, a long-standing board member with expertise in capital raising, White Gold is well on its way to becoming a major gold producer. As the share price rises, questions may arise about the company's ability to raise additional capital at a valuation above $0.85/share. Nevertheless, White Gold has a solid base of large investors, institutions, and private equity firms eager to support the next phase of development. The strength of the gold market remains a key driver of interest in White Gold's projects, particularly with regard to the progress on its open-pit mining plans. As the share price gains momentum, the chances of further capital inflows increase, in our view. As White Gold continues to execute its strategy and improve its outlook, the shares will not appear overvalued. Instead, in light of the climbing Au price and the company's direction, they will still look attractive, thus creating the conditions to attract even more investors.

The Risks

These are the risk factors and the de-risk factors.

The Empowering Of Leading Strategic Infrastructures

White Gold will benefit from the construction of road networks and energy supply infrastructure, as well as from the expansion of maritime transport platforms and infrastructure for the extraction of critical metals.

Private Ownership Bring In the Funds

Sufficient financial resources are available: White Gold Corp. raised a total of $1.7 million to finance its core business in the twelve months ending September 2025. During the same period, the company invested $2.2 million. Assuming an annual financing requirement of at least $4 million, White Gold raised approximately $23 million from investors through a private placement in October 2025. This equates to $23.5 million in cash and cash equivalents (including $0.5 million in funds existing at September 30, 2025).

We also believe that the “private placement” used to raise capital, which was successfully completed after being scaled up, is evidence that supports the idea that these strategic investors consider the $23 million raised as sufficient capital for White Gold’s planned projects.

The Low Daily Trading Volume Could Be Problematic

Liquidity in US OTC for White Gold Corp.'s shares is low: The average volume (3 months) is 115,302 at the time of writing (see Seeking Alpha in the “Trading Data” section). Low daily trading volume can cause problems and force investors to accept lower selling prices if they suddenly need to sell shares due to unforeseen circumstances. This can lead to poor performance or even losses.

On the other hand, low daily trading volume can also create upward pressure if many market participants suddenly express interest in investing in the company's shares at the same time.

The Risk Of Bearish Gold Price

For example, if the Federal Reserve does not cut US interest rates this year, contrary to general expectations, or only cuts them slightly, this could affect the price of gold per ounce. A slowdown in global geopolitical tensions, which dampens demand for gold as a safe haven, could also affect the price of gold per ounce. The market could then view White Gold's mineral project as less appealing, which could potentially put downward pressure on the share price. But at the moment, central banks are heavily accumulating gold bullion in their vaults, and we do not expect this scenario with falling gold prices. But as you know, nothing is certain in the markets.

In one of our earlier articles, we wrote:

Driven by the need to reckon with and confront a global environment that will never be ideal for economic stability and geopolitical peace, the current situation after about three years of heading towards a gold rush is that these underlying forces are now poised to further catalyze the price of precious metals.

Conclusion

White Gold Corp., or WHGOF, listed on the US OTCQX, is enjoying increasing market confidence. The company's flagship project in the emerging White Gold District of the Yukon Territory in northwestern Canada continues to deliver excellent drill results, demonstrating continuity, extent, and high-grade deposits. The company is benefiting from the upward trend in the gold price and is building its management expertise in converting exploration results into productive deposits and securing the necessary capital. The spin-out of the copper and critical metals businesses underscores management's strong commitment to realizing the gold project—a commitment reflected in the company name.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: MLPs Vs. Midstream Corporates: How AMZA Seeks To Capture Income Opportunities
data.description:
Midstream energy companies operate much like toll roads, collecting fees for transporting oil and natural gas rather than relying on commodity prices. That fee-based model has long appealed to income investors, but corporate structure plays a decisive role in how much cash ultimately reaches shareholders. Midstream firms generally operate as traditional C-corporations or as master limited partnerships (MLPs), and the distinction can materially affect income received. Many pipeline corporations offer dividends in the mid-single digits, while established MLPs have historically delivered meaningfully higher distributions.¹

The difference stems largely from taxation. MLPs typically avoid corporate income tax, allowing more operating cash flow to be passed directly to investors.¹ Corporations, by contrast, are taxed at the entity level before distributing dividends. The trade-off is complexity. Investors in MLPs are treated as partners and receive Schedule K-1 tax forms, which can complicate filings and discourage participation.¹ These frictions have kept some income-focused investors on the sidelines despite the sector’s attractive yields.

Structural Change and the Natural Gas Shift

The midstream sector has evolved meaningfully over the past decade. Once characterized by complex partnership structures and incentive distribution rights, many operators have simplified governance, reduced leverage, and aligned payouts with sustainable cash flow. Some firms have even converted from MLPs to corporate structures to broaden their investor base.¹

What has not changed is the underlying business model. Pipelines earn fees based on volumes transported under long-term contracts, insulating cash flows from short-term commodity price volatility. That stability has become more pronounced as the industry pivots toward natural gas. Roughly three-quarters of North American midstream infrastructure now centers on natural gas rather than oil, reflecting demand from power generation, residential heating, and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports.²

Natural gas demand tends to be less cyclical than oil consumption, and the expansion of U.S. LNG exports has created an additional long-term demand channel.² At the same time, management teams have become more disciplined, funding capital projects internally and prioritizing balance sheet strength. Despite these improvements, many midstream companies continue to trade below historical valuation averages, suggesting that the market has yet to fully recognize the sector’s durability.²

How AMZA Packages Midstream Income

The InfraCap MLP ETF (AMZA, the Fund) aims to bridge the gap between high-yield MLP income and investor convenience. Rather than owning individual partnerships directly, investors gain diversified exposure through a single ETF and receive a standard Form 1099-DIV instead of multiple K-1s.³

AMZA is actively managed, allowing portfolio managers to adjust holdings based on fundamentals and market conditions rather than tracking a static index. The Fund typically holds a diversified mix of major MLPs and related midstream companies. To help enhance income, AMZA employs moderate leverage and selectively writes covered call options, generating additional cash flow while supporting monthly distributions.⁴ These tools can increase income potential but may also amplify volatility during periods of market stress.

For investors comfortable with those trade-offs, AMZA offers a streamlined way to access the steady cash flows of U.S. energy infrastructure. It transforms a structurally complex segment of the market into a single, tradeable vehicle while preserving the core attraction of midstream investing: potentially durable income backed by essential assets.

data.title: BigBear.Ai: Strategic Shift Emerging, Execution Still Unproven (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
BigBear.ai (BBAI) has transitioned from a valuation-excess stage in October 2025, when I issued a Sell call. The share prices are down ~40% since, and I think it is a time to book profits and move to a Hold. The warrant overhang is a lesser mechanical pressure now. The risks of further dilutive events have shifted from existential funding to more growth investments needs. An Ask Sage acquisition also makes ~20% revenue uptick possible in 2026.

BigBear is still not a Buy because execution and integration risks remain. As of now, we do not have any proof of a rebound in core revenue. The profitability situation has also remained where it was in October. Also, a lack of early stage traction does not rule out further multiple contraction.

Growth and Profitability Concerns Stay

With Q3 data in, the revenue contraction issues are still somewhat at play. Somewhat, because there hasn't been further sequential decline. The guidance for the full year is a wide range from $125m-$140m and that is below the TTM revenue. Core organic revenue growth is missing - for an early stage defense-focused AI contractor, the proof of concept is often lumpy and requires years of build and months of sales cycle. BigBear had also run into hurdles like an untimely government shutdown, further delaying any possible traction. However, fact is, traction so far is not strong enough to call the product-market fit validated yet. That's the bigger concern - actual revenue trends matter less at this stage, but de-growth signs do not help BigBear's cause.

The street already embeds a 20-25% growth in 2026. It will be important to see how BigBear guides ahead in the upcoming Q4 quarterly call and whether this estimate gets an upgrade. Organic growth remains as critical as the overall growth outlook for the next year. As of now, the ~$30m revenue uptick in 2026, broadly matches with the ~$25m ARR from the Ask Sage acquisition. Integration timelines and organic growth beyond the Ask Sage addition will be critical to gauge.

The significant EBITDA losses I had noted in October also persist in Q3. Adjusted EBITDA was reported as -$9.4m against $900k in Q3 2024. There is no sign of any near term margin inflection either. Gross margins declined. Margin improvements ahead will depend on SG&A efficiencies post-integration and growth investment outlook.

Some Strategic Changes

The revenue and profitability trends don't inspire an upgrade, because there is only a pause in terms of further sequential decline. Where the upgrade to a Hold starts emerging is a potential business model transformation at play. Bigbear's earlier profile was that of a AI-enabled defense contractor - that is, lumpy and project based revenue, modest margins and a services-heavy mix. The Ask Sage acquisition introduces recurring revenue. The integration risks aside, this is going to immediately impact revenue by ~20%, mostly in a recurring format that smooths the overall revenue profile.

The magnitude is not a game changer yet, per se, but there are a few factors that call for watching the synergy benefits ahead. For one, the ~$25m ARR is already a six-fold increase in Ask Sage's topline over the past year alone. The growth is visibly strong here and even if it slows ahead, the revenue contributions could easily become material over the course of the next two years. While early stage growth dynamics are difficult to predict and do not necessarily scale at same multiples, a 2x jump next year and that followed by say 50% in the second year alone, takes revenue contributions to ~$75m annually- that is more than half Bigbear's current annual revenue. This is a purely hypothetical path I have outlined just for context.

There are other factors beyond revenue impact too that need some watching. One is gross margins could be healthy in this segment and that could mean overall better gross margins for BigBear - something the next few quarters could reveal after integration completes. Cross-selling opportunities may also open up. Also, although this may not be transformational right away, it signals a strategic shift. The revenue profile now has a recurring element in it. This could also signal a strategic shift to include secure GenAI infrastructure into BigBear's core - further inorganic additions (platform style acquisitions) or internal builds in this direction is not ruled out.

Dilution Concerns Lower But Exists

Another factor that looks less restrictive today is the effect of dilution and warrants. It is not yet a very optimistic capital structure story, but there are some nuanced improvements to the situation in October. Based on the Q3 Form 10-Q, the share count has increased ~73% YTD as of Q3 data (FY2025). Since Q3 though, the share count has remained stable. Some of the primary contributors toward this dilution are the ATM issuance of ~143m shares and another ~17m shares from 2029 note conversions. Around 15m shares came from warrant exercises.

The mechanical ceiling presented by the warrants is less restrictive today after share price corrections. Legacy IPO warrants carry a $11.5 strike, which is looking quite far away from the ~$4 levels BigBear is trading at today. So, it is not dilutive in the near term, unless the stock trades above the strike. In fact, the primary dilution pressures now appear to lie in convertibles, not warrants - especially the 2029 conversion, which has around $144m tied to it.

Future dilution events should be contained now though, as the cash position has drastically improved over the past couple of quarters. This is however, subject to how management wants to deploy growth investments and whether any further acquisitions in recurring platform plays (like Ask Sage) are in play. Management has remained open to fund growth through equity issuance, so capital structure may remain a relevant topic going ahead too, although the cash position looks better positioned now.

Booking Profits Ahead of Q4

The most important factor behind booking profits and moving BigBear to a watch and Hold stage is that valuations have compressed significantly from ~20x sales to around ~11x sales. Although there could be room for further compression from these levels, the Ask Sage acquisition and recurring revenue mix and a reduced dilution threat perception ahead, increase odds of a rerating ahead, after meaningful compression.

I therefore recommend booking profits on shorts initiated in October and awaiting Q4 commentary for further directions. The most important metric that I will keep an eye on is FY2025 closing revenue. The YTD revenue is ~$100m. Assuming no material sequential deterioration, i.e. ~$30m Q4 revenue, the full year revenue lands just around the midpoint of the guidance ($125m-$140m). If revenue tracks the lower end of the guidance range, that would imply further topline deceleration sequentially and encourage another phase of selling. On the other hand, if revenue comes out toward the higher end of the range, that could signal a rebound and keep the Hold stance intact.

I will also await any guidance around integration timelines for Ask Sage, and how management guides the next financial year growth. If the 2026 guidance shows some organic growth beyond inorganic addition from Ask Sage, we could evaluate a Buy too.

data.title: eToro: The Rebound Is Just Beginning As Net Inflows Rise
data.description:
Alongside the sharp fall in software stocks in 2026, the other major decliner that has driven capital flight is the sharp fall in the price of Bitcoin, which is still (at the time of writing) struggling to find support at the ~$70k mark and has lost ~30% over the past month alone.

That slide has taken down many stocks in the crypto ecosystem as well, even those whose direct exposure to falling prices isn't severe. eToro (ETOR), a brokerage company that began in Europe and has recently expanded to the U.S., is one of the major stocks that has been caught up in crypto-price pessimism, even though commissions from crypto trading only form a small chunk of its business. eToro recently jumped 20%+ after reporting strong Q4 results, though this jump only partially offsets a steep decline relative to share prices near $60 closer to the company's IPO last year. In my view, this is the beginning of a far more substantial rebound in this underappreciated company.

I last wrote a strong buy article on eToro in January, when the stock was trading in the low $30s. Note that I rarely assign a rating of "strong buy" to any stock, since to me that indicates only the companies in which I have the highest conviction from both a fundamentals and valuation perspective. Post-Q4 earnings, my conviction in eToro has only risen, especially with the company hinting at an expanding product flywheel for 2026 that will help to accelerate net fund inflows, further diversifying the company from any concentrated crypto risks. I remain at a strong buy rating here.

There are a couple of recent factors that investors need to keep in mind here when considering a buy on eToro. The first is that while eToro has been heavily associated with crypto trading, it has done a fantastic job at diversification recently. In Q4, its net contribution from crypto (as a reminder, net contribution is the company's trading revenue less direct trading costs) fell to just 24%, down from 50% in the year-ago quarter (and meanwhile, while eToro's revenue and net contribution fell, it fell only in the low double digits and came ahead of expectations - more details in the next section). In short, I think a large chunk of eToro's post-earnings gains is coming from investors realizing that eToro is well equipped to sustain growth and profitability even as crypto markets remain shaky.

We note as well that eToro has many product catalysts to drive growth as well. The snapshot below showcases all of the company's recent launches, as well as the products it has on the roadmap for 2026:

Several of these new features are of particular note. First, the company has been emphasizing targeting "neo banking" and savings account features on a market-by-market basis. For example, in France the company launched retirement accounts and life insurance products, and in the UK it added support from ISAs (individual savings accounts). We note that these efforts have helped to grow the company's net inflows. Also noteworthy is eToro's growing focus on its App Store and allowing users to build third-party applications directly within the eToro ecosystem. Over time, we think marketplace fees can become a meaningful, recurring source of software revenue for the company.

All in all, eToro remains an incredibly compelling company. Keep riding the post-earnings rally higher.

Q4 Download

Let's now go through eToro's latest quarterly results in greater detail. The Q4 net contribution trends are shown below:

Total net contribution fell -10% y/y to $227 million. As a reminder, net contribution had an especially tough comp against heightened crypto trading activity in Q4 of FY24, owing to the surge in crypto-related assets on the promise of deregulation after Trump was re-elected to the White House. The tough comp suffered a double whammy from price volatility in the past few months, as hardened crypto investors typically take the philosophy of "HODL" (hold on for dear life) when prices decline, reducing trading activity overall. Still, we note that net contribution from equities and commodity trading grew 45% y/y to $116 million, and despite market volatility in Q4, net contribution also grew 6% sequentially from Q3. Wall Street had only expected $218 million in net contribution in the quarter, or roughly flat quarter over quarter.

What continues to impress me is that in the wake of market volatility, eToro's expanding product flywheel plus its U.S. launch continues to catalyze new deposits into the platform. Total money transfers grew 29% y/y to $3.7 billion in the quarter, with the company also adding ~80k net-new funded accounts to end the quarter with 3.81 million accounts.

And so despite the erosion in value in crypto assets, we note that net deposits have still helped eToro to grow its assets under administration by 11% y/y to $18.5 billion. A growing chunk of this AUA is in cash ($3.6 billion, +20% y/y). We note that eToro's growing cash balances have also helped the company to actually increase its net interest income (up 18% y/y to $59 million), despite the headwinds from falling interest rates.

The company continues to plan on stepping up its marketing in 2026 to aid its recent U.S. launch. Per CEO Jonathan Assia's remarks on the Q&A portion of the Q4 earnings call on the company's progress in the U.S.:

So we've been very active since the IPO in expanding eToro's U.S. product launches and product road map since then we've launched CopyTrader. We plan to launch in H1 this year, Smart Portfolios, which is based on our RIA license, which is in process. We're looking at prediction markets as well. For the U.S., we've seen a significant uptake as we started scaling some of the marketing activities from Q4 to Q1. So we are seeing a significant uptick to something that's still early stages of the U.S. business, but we're very excited about continuing to launch all of eToro's global product road map here in the U.S. I do believe that now with being able to accelerate product development, we'll be able to actually launch more of our products than we expected originally in 2026 here in the U.S.

And we're making sure we are, I'd say, selective in how we scale our marketing budget, here in the U.S., we want to see CAC to LTV, while not at the same levels as globally, we want to see that ratio between CAC to LTV increase. We've seen the best ratio we've seen to date last year, especially, by the way, moving forward into the year. And that basically gives us the ability to continue and scale up marketing activity and then funded accounts where we believe product engagement will then follow as well."

We note that healthy trading activity has continued into January. Yes, it's true that crypto trades fell -50% y/y (with average trade amounts also falling -34% y/y, implying ~$728 million in net trading activity, down ~67% y/y). But non-crypto trades rose 55% y/y to 74 million trades at an average investment of $252 per trade (+8% y/y), implying $18.65 billion in non-crypto trading activity that grew 66% y/y. We're hoping that the strength in equities activity will help investors shift their opinion on eToro and reduce its correlation to overall crypto market status.

eToro also managed $87 million in adjusted EBITDA in Q4 at a 38% margin on net contribution dollars, slightly down from 43% in the year-ago Q4. Still, we note the company managed adjusted EBITDA growth on a full-year basis to $317 million (+4% y/y).

Valuation, Risks, and Key Takeaways

Forward valuations are tough for eToro because the company doesn't provide guidance (and for good reason: it's tough to predict the markets and trading activity overall). That said, even using conservative estimates, it's clear to see that eToro remains a deep value stock.

At current share prices near $33, the company trades at a $2.77 billion market cap. Netting off $1.33 billion in cash and crypto assets against $107.8 million in deposits owed to users gives us an enterprise value of $1.55 billion.

This represents just a 4.9x EV/FY25 adjusted EBITDA multiple against trailing adjusted EBITDA. We expect that with new market launches, especially in the U.S., net deposits fueling net interest income, and continued strong equity trading trends, the company should be able to grow net contribution and adjusted EBITDA in FY26 (consensus is currently calling for 8% growth in net contribution levels to $936 million on a full-year basis).

Of course, there are also risks to keep in mind here. The major wildcard for eToro is its U.S. launch, where the company is up against both established brokerages as well as the rising popularity of Robinhood (HOOD). Even Coinbase (COIN), in an attempt to diversify from crypto, has also announced support for stock trading. eToro's attempts to break into the world's largest and most important investment market come as a late entrant and may not be successful.

That said, with strong deposit inflows and a recently boosted share buyback program (with $150 million now open on its authorization, or 5% of its current market cap), I see more positives than negatives for eToro. Stay long here post-earnings.

data.title: Seaboard: New Vessels Hitting The Water While Pork Remains Strong
data.description:
Introduction

While I generally don’t like companies or holding companies with a majority shareholder, it usually also means there is a stronger incentive to perform well. In Seaboard Corporation's (SEB) case, the activities are quite diversified (pork, turkey, marine services, agricultural trading services), while the focus remains on keeping the balance sheet very strong despite investing in growth initiatives. The stock is now up over 40% since I last discussed the company in February 2024.

The company recently released its financial results for FY 2025, and as its quarterly updates are usually pretty short, the full annual report provides an interesting look under the hood.

A look at the full-year results

Seaboard is active in several different segments. The pork division is a vertically integrated pork producer and is Seaboard’s most capital-intensive division, as it represents 41% of the company’s total assets and 37% of all inventory levels. The CT&M segment includes commodity trading, processing, and logistics services to the agricultural sector.

While the latter represents a total revenue of almost $5.2 billion, it’s a low-margin division with operating margins below 3%. That’s also the case for the pork segment, where the operating income in 2025 came in at $67 million on a $2.02 billion revenue, but that was an improvement from the $20 million in operating income on a $2.06 billion revenue in 2024.

The smaller divisions have higher margins. The marine segment (cargo shipping services) saw its operating margin increase to exceeding 10%, while the power segment saw a $46 million operating income on a $232 million revenue. And while that is a decrease compared to the results in 2024 and 2025, the power segment (the production of electricity in the Dominican Republic) remains an important division for Seaboard as it has earmarked a $150 million investment budget in 2026 for the construction of EDM IV, a new barge project in the Dominican Republic. The total investment in EDM IV is expected to be $315 million, and the project will be operational in 2028. The power plant will have gas and steam turbines, which will be more efficient than the older plants like EDM II. Seaboard is looking into its options to perhaps monetize EDM II once EDM IV is operational.

Taking all these elements into consideration, the total revenue came in at $9.75 billion in 2025 (and $2.4 billion in Q4), resulting in a substantial increase in its gross income, which jumped by about 20% to $693 million.

After also deducting the higher SG&A expenses, the consolidated operating income was a very respectable $239 million, a 50% higher result compared to FY 2024. Additionally, the income from affiliates (mainly the 52.5% stake in the Butterball turkey brand) also increased, resulting in a pre-tax income of $410 million and a net profit of $501 million after also recording an income tax benefit. The net profit attributable to the common shareholders of Seaboard was $496 million, which works out to an EPS of just under $515 based on the weighted average share count of just over 964,000 shares. Seaboard continues to buy back its own shares, and the current share count has decreased to just under 958,000 shares.

Looking at the company’s cash flow performance, we see a reported operating cash flow of $568 million, but this includes a net investment of $100 million in the working capital position. We should also deduct the $38 million in lease payments, resulting in an underlying operating cash flow of $630 million.

The company invested about $562 million, so its net free cash flow was relatively low, but keep in mind that the majority of the capital expenditures are geared towards further growth. After all, the total amount of depreciation and amortization expenses is less than $320 million, while the combination of capex and lease payments came in at almost twice that amount.

Seaboard disclosed that about $302 million of the cash capex was related to making down payments on vessels under construction. Six new vessels were delivered during 2025, and an additional new vessel was ordered in 2025.

Seaboard’s balance sheet remains strong. At the end of December, the company had $1.23 billion in cash and short-term investments, while its current liabilities include $458 million in line of credit debt and $977 million in long-term debt for a total net debt of around $200 million.

The stock is currently trading close to its tangible book value, but you could argue that the fair value of the investments in affiliates is higher than the $795 million book value.

Investment thesis

It will be interesting to see how the company’s marine segment performs in the current financial year, and I hope the recently delivered vessels will perform well. I don’t expect Seaboard to generate free cash flow this year as the company continues to invest hundreds of millions of dollars to increase its asset base. But with plenty of liquid assets on the balance sheet and a positive working capital position of exceeding $2 billion, Seaboard can easily take care of the investments. The dividend remains very low ($2.25 per share per quarter), and most cash flow is reinvested.

I currently have no position in Seaboard, but I am keeping an eye on the company and its progress.
